channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Russ Vought, tapped as CFPB's acting director, directs bureau to issue no new rules, stop new investigations
item.description: Russell Vought, acting director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, has directed staff to not issue any new rules and to stop any new investigations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans mull taxes on scholarships and changes to student loan programs
item.description: As Republicans in Congress look for ways to slash spending, some legislators are floating new taxes on college scholarships, an end to student loan repayment plans and a big hike in taxes on university endowments.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne Mumphrey, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Musk says he has no plans to buy TikTok
item.description: Despite ByteDance stating that they are not planning to sell, some investors have shown interest in buying the company.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE siege of USAID dominated week in politics
item.description: USAID operations have effectively been put on hold, less than three weeks after Trump began his second term in office.
item.dc:creator: Mills Hayes, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's 3rd week saw more executive orders, a trade war that wasn't and a Mideast jolt
item.description: President Donald Trump keeps cranking out executive orders designed to remake the government while his ally and adviser, billionaire Elon Musk, hunts for more ways to upend the federal workforce.
item.dc:creator: By Will Weissert And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Spending bill talks bog down after Trump's efforts to slash government
item.description: Talks on a spending bill for the current budget year have bogged down in the wake of President Donald Trump’s efforts to slash federal spending and its workforce.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Here’s a look at the candidates in Michigan’s governor race so far
item.description: Some high-profile names have already launched their campaigns in the race for Michigan’s governor in 2026.
item.dc:creator: Samantha Sayles, Local 4

item.title: Trump signs order to establish White House Faith Office
item.description: In the order, Trump directed the office to identify ways to reduce burdens on the free exercise of religion.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Dem AGs sue over DOGE access to Treasury payment systems
item.description: “As the richest man in the world, Elon Musk is not used to being told ‘no,’ but in our country, no one is above the law,” James said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing 2,200 USAID workers on leave
item.description: A federal judge on Friday ordered a temporary block on efforts by the Trump administration to place thousands of USAID employees on leave.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says he's firing Kennedy Center board of trustees members and naming himself chairman
item.description: President Donald Trump says he's firing members of the board of trustees for the John F. Kennedy Center for the Performing Arts and naming himself chairman.
item.dc:creator: Will Weissert, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Top federal agency with history of wasteful spending could be next DOGE target
item.description: Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) efforts appear ready to shift focus to the Social Security Administration, which is likely to spark criticisms from Democrats similar to those in response to DOGE's moves against the U.S. Agency for International Development (USAID).
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump tells Ishiba at the White House that he wants to slash the US trade deficit with Japan
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Friday he wants to slash the U.S. trade deficit with Japan as he welcomed Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba to the White House for their first face-to-face meeting.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More active duty troops will head to US-Mexico border, bringing the total to 3,600
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon will deploy roughly 1,500 more active duty soldiers to the southern border to support President Donald Trump’s expanding crackdown on immigration, a U.S. official said Friday. That would eventually bring the total to about 3,600 active duty troops at the border.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk says he'll bring back DOGE staffer under fire for racist posts
item.description: Elon Musk said Friday he will rehire a former Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) staffer who resigned after several of the aide's racist social media posts were exposed. "He will be brought back," Musk posted on X, the social media platform he purchased in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, NewsNation

item.title: Musk team’s access to student loan systems raises alarm over borrowers' personal information
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Democrats are pushing back against Elon Musk’s Department of Government Efficiency as it turns its attention to the Education Department, with lawmakers raising concerns about DOGE’s access to internal systems containing personal information on tens of millions of Americans.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and BIANCA VÁZQUEZ TONESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Kash Patel had a roster of foreign clients. Their interests could clash with FBI he hopes to lead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Kash Patel, President Donald Trump’s pick to lead the FBI, holds more than $1 million of stock in a fashion company founded in China. He established a nonprofit that spent big on promotion but little on its mission.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN SLODYSKO, ERIC TUCKER and ALAN SUDERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump administration withdraws support for transgender minors in Tennessee case at the Supreme Court
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The federal government no longer backs transgender minors and their families in Tennessee who are challenging a state ban on gender-affirming care, the Trump administration told the Supreme Court Friday.
item.dc:creator: MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP unveils $340B budget plan with Trump's deportation and defense funds, as House stalls
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — As House Republicans missed another deadline Friday to produce a massive budget package of tax cuts and slashed spending, Senate Republicans jumped ahead, unveiling a more tailored $340 billion blueprint focused on President Donald Trump's deportation agenda and bolstered U.S.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Are Americans paying attention to Musk and Trump's DOGE? Here's what people in Iowa had to say
item.description: URBANDALE, Iowa (AP) — Iowa Sen. Joni Ernst has hailed Elon Musk and the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency as a necessary force in Washington, D.C., calling it “a storm that is headed this way that will sweep over this city and forever alter the way it operates.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judges curb Trump’s sweeping executive actions
item.description: Federal judges are curbing President Trump’s sweeping directives to reshape the government, issuing a flurry of rulings blocking his agenda from charging forward.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Tribal colleges and universities react to executive order rescinding support efforts for Indigenous education
item.description: The Trump administration’s sweeping orders could force some tribal colleges and universities to shut down, school leaders said.
item.dc:creator: MPR News

item.title: Arab, Muslim Americans blast Trump's 'grotesque' Gaza proposal
item.description: Arab American and Muslim leaders are blasting President Trump’s proposal to take over Gaza, warning that it’s antithetical to their beliefs and amounts to ethnic cleansing.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: New Minnesota House Speaker Lisa Demuth on priorities, vision for 2025 session
item.description: Under a new power-sharing deal, Demuth will be speaker at least through next year. She spoke with MPR News host Cathy Wurzer.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Gretchen Brown, Ngoc Bui, and Jeff Jones, MPR News

item.title: Lawmakers denied entry into Department of Education
item.description: The doors were locked, and a security guard could be seen having a tense conversation with a lawmaker.
item.dc:creator: Jackie Koppell, NewsNation

item.title: Judge blocks Trump from placing thousands of USAID workers on leave and giving them 30-day deadline
item.description: A federal judge has dealt President Donald Trump and billionaire ally Elon Musk their first big setback in their dismantling of the U.S. Agency for International Development, ordering a temporary halt to plans to pull thousands of agency staffers off the job.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Knickmeyer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Race for NYC mayor heats up as primary opponents hit Adams on education, immigrants
item.description: ALBANY, N.Y. (PIX11) -- During a budget hearing in front of the state legislature Tuesday, two Democrats looking to unseat fellow Democratic Mayor Eric Adams took their shot. State Sen.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11

item.title: Bill count swells to 484 as Nevada Legislature hits Day 2; Voter ID a Republican goal
item.description: Packed binders. Busy halls. Lobbyists, lawmakers and journalists roaming amongst each other. This is when the routine of the state government sets in. Already, there are 484 bills submitted this session, with many more expected.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada Senate was a battleground in 2023, new Republican minority leader emphasizes setting priorities
item.description: The Nevada Senate is ready to get down to business, and the end of the 2023 Legislature is still fresh in the minds of Democrats and Republicans gathered in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: John Langeler, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: NJ bill looks to give veteran nonprofits a boost
item.description: UNION, N.J. (PIX11) - Almost any veteran non-profit organization would tell you that more money means more critical services they can provide to those who've served our country. New Jersey Assemblywoman Tennille McCoy is authoring a bill that would hope to give certain veteran nonprofits a hand.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Florida immigration bill, marijuana petition spark debate
item.description: The battle between Gov. Ron DeSantis and state GOP lawmakers has become more public and continues to heat up.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
